objective: Map Cursor (IDE) workflows so workers can safely navigate codebases, edit files, search, run commands, and use built-in tools without destructive actions.
automation_hierarchy: If a direct API exists for the task, use that (WEB_API skill).
If no API exists but the task is a safe file transform, create a Python Sandbox skill (metadata.sandbox) and run it in E2B.
If neither exists, fall back to UI automation via clicking/typing.
constraints: Work only in a dedicated test workspace/repo; do not modify unrelated user projects
Do not push to remotes, publish extensions, or change global Git config
Avoid destructive operations (mass delete, reformat entire repo) unless explicitly instructed
Prefer stable UI selectors (Explorer tree, Command Palette, editor tabs, terminal panel)
If a command runs code, prefer read-only/safe commands first (e.g., tests, lint) and avoid anything that deletes data

search_refactor: rename symbol (in a small safe sample file)
editing: format document (only if repo has formatter configured)
terminal_tasks: run a harmless command (e.g., listing files) in the integrated terminal
git_safe: create a local commit in a test repo (optional, if safe and explicitly allowed)
